content.summary_short: What ByteCast is, how the Golden Path actually runs today, what each main door is for, and why proof-based progression beats random wandering.
content.summary_long: EP-001 anchors the current Day 1 journey with concrete surfaces and sequence: Workspace Entry for orientation, Playlist for resume and next step, Training Hub for routes, Seed Builder for repeatable output, and Docs for governed answers. The learner leaves knowing the real Golden Path order from EP-001 through TR-001A, Seed export, publish, and badge, plus why the system records proof instead of assuming understanding from clicks alone. Voiceover: docs/day1/EP-001_VOICEOVER.md. On-screen spec: docs/day1/EP-001_ON_SCREEN.md.

slides.title: What ByteCast is
slides.goal: Understand the layer you are standing in before you touch deeper systems.
bullets: ByteCast is a connected training layer built from focused packs and thin routing shells, not one giant app.
The system already has a real Day 1 path: EP-001, EP-002, EP-003, EP-004, then TR-001A before seed work opens.
That shape matters because the learner is being guided toward understanding, proof, and repeatable work, not just exposure.

slides.title: The journey loop
slides.goal: See the actual learning contract, not just the slogan.
bullets: Listen -> Slides -> Engage builds orientation first; Training and Seed come after the early proof gates.
On the current Golden Path, the loop continues into TR-001A, Seed export, publish link capture, and badge eligibility.
The system stores step proof so returning after a break does not depend on memory or sticky notes.

slides.title: Path, not a pile
slides.goal: Build the reflex to return to the path when the map feels noisy.
bullets: A pile says open more tabs; a path says check the Playlist, confirm your step, and move one honest action forward.
Beginner progress is not measured by how many internal tools you saw. It is measured by whether you can stay correctly routed.
ByteCast is training judgment as much as content: know what comes next, and know what can wait.

slides.title: The five main doors
slides.goal: Match real surfaces to real jobs.
bullets: Workspace Entry is the front door for orientation; ByteCast Playlist is the learner surface for discovery, resume, and next action.
Training Hub maps routes and missions; Seed Builder Studio handles templates, generators, and repeatable artifact work.
Docs Portal is where governed references live, including standards, proof rules, troubleshooting, and site structure.

slides.title: Learner-facing vs internal
slides.goal: Understand why some surfaces are visible but still not your Day 1 starting point.
bullets: Public learning doors exist so new learners do not have to infer workflow from raw registries or maintainer screens.
Files like journey config, workflow data, or tooling internals are real; they are just not the first lens for a new operator.
The discipline is simple: use learner-safe surfaces until the map and truth rules are stable in your head.

slides.title: Canonical starts
slides.goal: Know why the episode order exists.
bullets: EP-001 teaches the learning system itself so later episodes land in the right context.
EP-002 explains the AeroVista ecosystem, EP-003 teaches which door handles which job, and EP-004 teaches where truth lives.
The sequence is intentional: system first, company second, navigation third, truth fourth.

slides.title: What progress means
slides.goal: Treat the locks as learning support, not friction.
bullets: Listen, slides, and Engage record whether the learner actually completed the early surface and passed the check.
TR-001A asks for stronger proof, including quiz performance and short written responses that a reviewer can audit later.
That is why the Golden Path feels sequential: it is designed to preserve meaning, not just movement.

slides.title: Success in EP-001
slides.goal: Know the operational bar for this episode.
bullets: You can explain ByteCast in plain language and name the full Golden Path from episode work into proof and seed output.
You can route three common needs: orientation to Entry, resume to Playlist, governed answer to Docs.
You know Training Hub is the map and Seed Builder is the repeatable-build door, not the default Day 1 landing spot.

slides.title: Beginner promise
bullets: You do not need every tool, every division, or every internal file on day one.
You do need a stable starting point, a clear route, and confidence that the system will tell you what comes next.
Good onboarding makes the environment feel navigable before it asks the learner to produce anything durable.

slides.title: Recap
bullets: ByteCast is the guided learning layer. The Golden Path is sequential and proof-based.
The five main doors each have a distinct job, and the Playlist is the safest default return surface.
Next up is EP-002, where the learner zooms out from the training shell into the AeroVista ecosystem itself.

items.text: Use Listen once, then finish the slide deck through Recap.
Open the Playlist and identify what it tells a learner about progress or next action.
Open Docs and name one kind of governed answer that belongs there.
Say aloud the current Golden Path order through TR-001A.

questions.explain: Focused packs and thin shells make the journey easier to route and maintain.

questions.q: If you return after a break and need the honest next step, the best first door is:
questions.explain: Playlist is built for discovery, resume, gates, and next recommended action.

choices: The only place lesson content exists
questions.explain: Hub organizes routes and modules; the actual learning packs live in their own surfaces.

questions.q: Docs Portal is the right first stop when you need:
choices: A governed standard or proof rule
To resume the next episode gate
questions.explain: Docs is the home for standards, governance, troubleshooting, and canon references.

choices: Stay on the path and let proof unlock the next layer
questions.explain: The sequence protects comprehension and makes later work easier, not harder.

aerovista_about.summary: AeroVista is a multi-division creative and engineering ecosystem shipping reusable experiences that blend software, storytelling, automation, music, and design.

divisions.name: NeXuS TechWorks
divisions.bread_and_butter: Full-stack engineering, offline-first apps, automation pipelines, DevOps, security hardening.

divisions.name: SkyForge Creative Studios
divisions.bread_and_butter: Interactive builds: playable sites, quests, AR/VR concepts, narrative experiences.

divisions.name: EchoVerse Audio
divisions.bread_and_butter: Music production, sound design, album worlds, audio tooling, interactive player drops.

divisions.name: Summit Learning
divisions.bread_and_butter: Teaching systems, tutorials, internal training, playbooks, skill paths.

divisions.name: Lumina Creative
divisions.bread_and_butter: Branding, visuals, marketing assets, launch kits, content strategy.

divisions.name: Vespera Publishing
divisions.bread_and_butter: Digital content, books/courses, structured knowledge products.

divisions.name: Horizon Aerial & Visual
divisions.bread_and_butter: Drone cinematography, mapping, 3D capture, real estate/ag/visual products.

primary.label: Open About AV
primary.note: Replace with your onboarding gate link when ready.

cta.recruitment_copy: Finish one clean pass through Listen, Slides, and Engage before exploring further. Clarity comes first.
